batch_summary: In a coffee shop, Phoebe is thrilled to learn that her brother, Frank Jr., and Alice have eloped. After celebrating their marriage, Phoebe asks what they would like as a wedding gift. Frank Jr. and Alice explain that they have been struggling to conceive and that their only option is IVF with a surrogate. They shock Phoebe by asking her to carry their baby. Stunned, Phoebe humorously notes she was thinking of getting them a gravy boat instead.
cumulative_summary: In a busy coffee shop, Phoebe is overjoyed to discover that her brother, Frank Jr., and his partner, Alice, have eloped after a spontaneous courthouse wedding. Phoebe warmly embraces the newlyweds, expressing her happiness at gaining a sister-in-law. However, the lighthearted mood shifts when Phoebe asks what they would like as a wedding gift. Frank Jr. and Alice reveal that they have been unsuccessfully trying to get pregnant and have learned they cannot conceive naturally. They explain that their only option to have a child is through in vitro fertilization (IVF) using a surrogate mother. In a surprising turn of events, they ask Phoebe if she would be willing to act as their surrogate and carry their baby. Stunned by the massive request, Phoebe is left momentarily speechless before humorously replying that she had been planning on gifting them a simple gravy boat instead.

batch_summary: Alice and Frank Jr. explain that they cannot afford a professional surrogate and prefer family over a stranger, urging Phoebe to take her time deciding. Meanwhile, at the apartment, Joey excitedly announces that Ross helped him land a job as a museum tour guide. Chandler tries to tease Joey about his blue blazer but fumbles the joke, leading to playful mockery from Monica and Rachel. Joey reassures the skeptical group that the job simply requires memorizing a script.

batch_summary: Phoebe excitedly shares the news of Frank Jr. and Alice's marriage and their request for her to be their surrogate. After clearing up Joey's hilarious misunderstanding about how surrogacy works, Phoebe expresses her eagerness to give them the ultimate gift. However, her friends urge caution. Rachel and Ross point out the intense physical and emotional challenges of pregnancy, such as morning sickness and labor, reminding her of the heavy toll it will take on her body for someone else's child.

batch_summary: As the group continues to express concern over Phoebe's surrogacy decision, Rachel suggests she talk to her mother. Phoebe decides to consult her birth mom, who actually gave birth. Meanwhile, Chandler shares a romantic moment with his girlfriend at Central Perk. Rachel and Monica envy their relationship phase, but Chandler reveals they haven't had sex yet, claiming he wants their love to grow first. However, after Ross skeptically calls him out, Chandler quickly admits he is lying about his noble intentions.

batch_summary: Phoebe visits her birth mother, Phoebe Sr., to discuss surrogacy. Phoebe Sr. strongly advises against it, warning her of the lifelong pain of giving up a child. To make her point, she gives Phoebe a pug puppy to look after for just three days, explaining that the heartbreak of returning the puppy is only a fraction of what it feels like to give up a baby. Phoebe is left to contemplate this emotional lesson.

batch_summary: Phoebe agrees to her mother's three-day puppy trial to test her emotional readiness for surrogacy. Meanwhile, at the museum cafeteria, Joey learns about a strict social divide from a fellow tour guide, Rhonda. Despite Rhonda's warnings and witnessing this elitist hierarchy firsthand, Joey rejects the division, firmly declaring that Ross is his best friend regardless of what they wear.
